findings.description: run_with_retries unconditionally resets attempt=0 at the top of every loop tail before incrementing, so attempt oscillates 0->1 forever and the `attempt < MAX_RETRIES` guard is never reached; a consistently-failing execute_job (failed convert, unknown kind, or raised exception) causes an infinite loop that never returns False and never increments self.failed.

Backoff is permanently stuck at RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (0+1) = 2.0s because attempt is reset to 0 on line 69 before this sleep, so the intended growing/exponential backoff never escalates (secondary effect of the line-69 reset bug).

poll_loop is `async def` but calls the blocking time.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS) instead of `await asyncio.sleep(...)`, blocking the entire event loop for 1s on every empty-queue poll and starving all other coroutines; asyncio is imported but never used.

poll_loop only evaluates the stop_after termination check after popping a non-None job, so poll_loop(stop_after=N) against an empty queue never reaches the return and loops forever; the documented stop condition is unreachable when no jobs arrive.

run_with_retries' time.sleep blocks the caller; invoked transitively from the async poll_loop (line 81) it blocks the event loop during backoff (and, combined with the line-69 infinite-loop bug, blocks it permanently on a failing job).

aggregate does sum(job.payload.get('values', [])) without validating element types; a JSON payload with non-numeric/string values raises TypeError, which (via the line-69 bug) is then caught and retried forever rather than failing the job.

An unknown/malformed job.kind returns False, which feeds directly into the infinite retry loop (line 69), making the permanent hang trivially reachable from untrusted queue input rather than terminating the job.

subprocess.run(f'convert {src} {dest}', shell=True) interpolates unsanitized job.payload values into a shell command, enabling command injection (e.g. dest='x; rm -rf /'); noted in passing per logic scope — defer full analysis to critic-security.
